title: Adopt Flow State into QMS Governance

comment:
Pre-review of CR-111 (Adopt Flow State into QMS Governance), v0.1 DRAFT.

VERIFICATION EVIDENCE:

Frontmatter:
- title: 'Adopt Flow State into QMS Governance' — present and meaningful.
- revision_summary: 'Initial draft' — present and appropriate for v0.1.

Required sections: All standard CR sections present (Purpose, Scope, Current State, Proposed State, Change Description, Justification, Impact Assessment, Testing Summary, Implementation Plan, Execution table, Execution Comments, Execution Summary, References). The omission of §7.4 (Development Controls) and §7.5 (Qualified State Continuity) is explicitly justified in §7.3 per TEMPLATE-CR guidance for document-only CRs — appropriate.

Placeholder content: The remaining bracket tokens ([SUMMARY], [Pass/Fail], [PERFORMER]-[DATE], [EXECUTION_SUMMARY], [COMMENT]) are confined to the Execution table and are expected to be populated during execution, not at draft time. No genuine placeholder content (TBD/TODO/FIXME) found in pre-execution sections.

Scope clarity: Five concrete deliverables enumerated in §2.2 (RS authoring, RTM authoring, qualified baseline recording, FLOW-STATE-1.0 tag, PROJECT_STATE update). Files affected enumerated in §2.3 and §7.1. Document-only character is unambiguous.

Internal consistency: §9 Implementation Plan maps cleanly to the EI-1 through EI-10 table. EI-1 and EI-10 capture pre/post-execution baselines per SOP-004 §5. The 12-requirement count is consistent across §2.2, §5.1 (5 domain groups), and §5.2.1 (12 anchor file rows).

VR flags: Document-only CR; no VR column populated, consistent with QMS-Policy §4.3 (VR not required for document-only changes).

Justification: §6 honestly addresses why a lightweight adoption is appropriate (mirrors SDLC-CQ pattern, avoids retrospective enumeration burden, allows requirements to grow organically on subsequent CRs).

Reviewer assignment: I have assigned tu_sketch, tu_sim, tu_scene, tu_ui — covering CAD, simulation, architecture/orchestration, and UI domains respectively. This matches the review team proposed in §9.2 of the CR itself.

MINOR OBSERVATION (non-blocking, author discretion):
§2.2 reads '12 high-level requirements across 4 architectural domains (ARCH, CAD, SIM, UI, plus one APP requirement)'. §5.1 and §5.2.1 consistently describe 5 domain groups (ARCH, CAD, SIM, UI, APP). The §2.2 framing is defensible (APP as application shell distinct from the four architectural domains) and the math is correct (4+3+2+2+1=12), but the author may wish to harmonize §2.2 with §5.1 for consistency. Not a blocker.

RECOMMENDATION: Recommend. CR-111 is well-structured, scope is clear, justification is sound, and the implementation plan is appropriately lightweight for a document-only adoption mirroring the SDLC-CQ precedent. Awaiting TU reviews before approval routing.

CAD/Geometry domain review: All seven requirements in my domain (REQ-FS-ARCH-001 through ARCH-004; REQ-FS-CAD-001 through CAD-003) are technically accurate, well-scoped, and verifiable by inspection against the cited anchor files. Verified against current flow-state/main: core/command_base.py defines the Command ABC with abstract execute()/undo() and the historize flag (ARCH-002 holds); core/scene.py has Scene.execute() as the sole mutation path (ARCH-001 holds); core/tool_context.py is the facade as described (ARCH-004 holds); model/sketch.py owns entities, constraints, drivers, materials, and interaction_data (CAD-001, CAD-003); model/solver.py provides dual solve()/solve_numba() paths and treats interaction_data as a constraint (CAD-002, CAD-003); model/solver_kernels.py contains the @njit PBD kernels (CAD-002). The lightweight adoption approach is appropriate and the SDLC-CQ precedent is well-applied. Recommend with one minor observation outside my primary domain that the author may want to consider for the RS text: REQ-FS-APP-001 lists the simulation execution mode as 'simulation', but flow-state/main.py declares the CLI choice as 'sim' (choices=['dashboard', 'sim', 'builder']). Since the RTM will verify by qualitative proof against the cited file, the requirement text and the file's actual behavior should match. This is TU-SIM/TU-SCENE territory and not a defect in my domain, so I am not blocking on it.

REQ-FS-UI-002 (Input Chain of Responsibility) misstates the layer order. The requirement says 'System -> Global -> Modal -> HUD', but the actual implementation in flow-state/ui/input_handler.py handle_input() (lines 113-129) processes events in the order System -> Modal -> Global -> HUD. The code has an explicit comment at line 117-118 stating this is intentional ('Modals BEFORE global hotkeys! Dialogs need to capture keyboard input before hotkeys trigger'). Since this is an Adoption CR capturing as-built invariants, the requirement should be corrected to match the implementation: 'System -> Modal -> Global -> HUD'. Note: CLAUDE.md Section 6.2 also has the wrong ordering and should be corrected (a separate doc-fix CR is appropriate). Without this fix, the RTM's qualitative-proof verification anchored to input_handler.py cannot honestly claim the file demonstrates the requirement as written. ALSO: REQ-FS-ARCH-003 (Scene/Session Split) lists 'interaction data' as state held by the Session, but the actual implementation stores interaction_data on sketch.interaction_data (see flow-state/core/tool_context.py set_interaction_data/clear_interaction_data and grep across flow-state for 'interaction_data' - it appears in scene.py, sketch.py, solver.py, tool_context.py, tools.py but NOT in session.py). The requirement should either drop 'interaction data' from the Session list or be reworded to acknowledge that interaction data is the live solver-target data carried on the sketch while the Session owns other transient view/selection state. Recommend dropping it from the Session list since the Session-side concern (focused_element, current_tool, selection, camera, mode, state, view options) is already comprehensive without it.

Reviewed from the Simulation domain. The two SIM requirements (REQ-FS-SIM-001 Particle Engine, REQ-FS-SIM-002 Compiler Bridge) accurately describe the existing system: simulation.py uses flat NumPy SoA arrays (pos_x, pos_y, vel_x, vel_y, force_x, force_y, is_static, etc.); physics_core.integrate_n_steps implements velocity Verlet (kick-drift-kick); build_neighbor_list and spatial_sort provide the uniform-grid spatial hash; engine/compiler.py is the one-way Sketch->Simulation bridge with a rebuild() entrypoint. Anchor files in the RTM map (engine/simulation.py, engine/physics_core.py, engine/compiler.py, core/scene.py) all exist at HEAD. Lightweight adoption strategy mirroring SDLC-CQ is appropriate given the no-tests-yet state and the goal of redirecting effort into Flow State feature work. Stylistic-only observation (not blocking): REQ-FS-SIM-002 says 'static Simulation atoms', but the Compiler also produces tethered atoms (is_static=3) for dynamic entities; future granular requirements may want to distinguish these. Recommend.

Three factual inaccuracies in proposed requirements that would lock incorrect statements into EFFECTIVE controlled documents. All within or adjacent to the orchestration/state-ownership domain (TU-SCENE).

(1) REQ-FS-APP-001 - incorrect CLI flag value. The proposed text names the three modes as 'dashboard', 'simulation', and 'builder'. The actual CLI value at flow-state/main.py:20 is 'sim', not 'simulation' (argparse choices=['dashboard', 'sim', 'builder']). The internal config constant is MODE_SIM. Either change the requirement to say 'sim' to match the code, or rephrase the requirement to name the modes conceptually (Dashboard mode, Simulation mode, Builder mode) without binding to a specific flag string. The current wording is a verifiable mismatch the RTM cannot honestly anchor.

(2) REQ-FS-UI-002 - incorrect input-layer order. The proposed text states 'System -> Global -> Modal -> HUD'. The actual implementation at flow-state/ui/input_handler.py:113-129 dispatches in order System -> Modal -> Global -> HUD, with an explicit in-source comment ('Modals (Context, Dialogs) - BEFORE global hotkeys! Dialogs need to capture keyboard input before hotkeys trigger'). CLAUDE.md Section 6.2 has the same inversion, but a controlled RS requirement should match the code, not the legacy documentation. Please correct the order in the requirement statement. (Note: CLAUDE.md may also need correcting in a follow-up, but that is not blocking for this CR.)

(3) REQ-FS-ARCH-003 - misclassification of interaction_data. The proposed text places 'interaction data' as Session (transient) state. In the actual code, interaction_data is stored on the Sketch object (flow-state/model/sketch.py:33: 'self.interaction_data = None'; tools write to self.sketch.interaction_data at flow-state/ui/tools.py:788, 978, 1061). The Sketch is owned by Scene, which the same requirement classifies as the persistent-state container. There is no interaction_data attribute on Session at all. This is a real architectural ambiguity in the codebase (transient data hanging off a persistent-state container) and the RS should not paper over it. Two acceptable resolutions: (a) drop 'interaction data' from the Session bullet list and either add a separate clause acknowledging that the solver's mouse-target buffer lives on Sketch as a transient field, or simply omit the example entirely; or (b) acknowledge it as a known asymmetry in the requirement text. Locking in the current phrasing would create a requirement the RTM cannot truthfully verify against the cited anchor (core/session.py).

Non-blocking observations (not grounds for request-updates, recorded for the author's awareness):
- REQ-FS-ARCH-001 'all mutations of persistent state shall flow through Command objects' is essentially correct, but the codebase does have internal propagation paths (MaterialManager.on_rebuild callback, Simulation.sync_entity_arrays) that mutate physics arrays as a downstream effect of commands rather than being commands themselves. The current wording is defensible (these are not user-initiated mutations), but if the Lead wants extra precision a phrase like 'user-initiated mutations of persistent Sketch/Simulation state' would close the gap.
- The RS intentionally omits an orchestration-loop requirement (the 8-step Scene.update sequence). That is consistent with the stated lightweight philosophy and is fine for v1.0; a future CR can add it when an orchestration-related change is made.

Recommend cleanly once items (1), (2), and (3) are addressed.

QA pre-review of CR-111 v0.2 (Adopt Flow State into QMS Governance). COMPLIANT — recommending. Verified items: (1) Frontmatter — title='Adopt Flow State into QMS Governance' (non-empty); revision_summary substantive, describing three v0.1→v0.2 factual corrections (REQ-FS-APP-001 mode wording, REQ-FS-UI-002 input layer order, REQ-FS-ARCH-003 Session contents) plus harmonized 5-domain-group wording, with a follow-up CR commitment for CLAUDE.md §2.3/§6.2 drift. (2) Structure — all CR sections 1–12 present; §7.4/§7.5 omission explicitly justified per TEMPLATE-CR guidance for document-only CRs. (3) Internal consistency — §2.2, §5.1, and §5.2.1 all agree on 12 requirements across 5 domain groups (ARCH×4 + CAD×3 + SIM×2 + UI×2 + APP×1 = 12); EI table EI-1..EI-10 maps cleanly onto §9 phases. (4) Placeholders — None in scope/description/justification; bracket tokens in §10 Execution table and §11 are the expected empty-execution skeleton for a PRE_REVIEW CR, not deficiencies. (5) Qualified Baseline — §5.3 correctly defers actual commit recording to RTM authoring at EI-5; the CR notes 'a26f7fb' only as expected, not as a locked-in claim. (6) Scope clarity — unambiguously document-only adoption (RS+RTM creation, git tag, PROJECT_STATE.md update); §7.3 confirms no submodule pointer change. (7) References — SOP-001/002/005/006, Quality-Manual 09/10, and SDLC-CQ precedent all plausibly cited. (8) Review team — proposed tu_sketch, tu_sim, tu_scene, tu_ui matches affected domains and has been assigned. No typos or formatting issues found.

CAD/Geometry domain review (TU-SKETCH): The four requirements touching the Sketch/Solver subsystem (REQ-FS-CAD-001/002/003, and the Sketch-relevant portion of REQ-FS-ARCH-003) are factually accurate against the code in flow-state/model/. Verified: (1) Sketch holds entities and constraints with lines and circles as the primary user-facing API (sketch.py); (2) the PBD solver provides both legacy OOP and Numba kernel paths switchable via the use_numba flag (solver.py + solver_kernels.py, toggled from input_handler.py); (3) the Interaction Servo treats the mouse target as a Phase-1 priority constraint injected each frame (solver._solve_interaction); (4) the v0.2 correction relocating interaction_data from Session to Sketch is correct - it is in fact a field on Sketch (sketch.py line 33). All anchor files cited in section 5.2.1 for the CAD rows exist at the expected paths. The high-level qualitative-proof posture is appropriate for an Adoption CR mirroring SDLC-CQ; deferring granular per-entity / per-tool / per-constraint requirements to future CRs is honest and well-justified. The 'e.g., parallel, perpendicular, equal, fixed' phrasing in REQ-FS-CAD-001 is non-exhaustive by construction and accurate. No defects, no scope gaps, no architectural concerns from the CAD domain perspective. Recommend.

UI-domain review (TU-UI). Verified the four UI-relevant requirements against current flow-state code: REQ-FS-UI-001 (UIManager retained tree + OverlayProvider) anchors at ui/ui_manager.py L70-120, ui/ui_widgets.py L31 (protocol) and L712 (Dropdown impl). REQ-FS-UI-002 (input order System -> Modal -> Global -> HUD) anchors at ui/input_handler.py L113-129, where the explicit comment 'Modals BEFORE global hotkeys' confirms the v0.2 correction is faithful to code; the modal-stack reset claim verifies at app/app_controller.py L59 and L75 where push_modal/pop_modal both call ui.root.reset_interaction_state(). REQ-FS-ARCH-004 (ToolContext facade) anchors at core/tool_context.py and exposes exactly the categories the requirement names (execute, view/geometry queries, transient interaction state); the underscore-prefixed _get_sketch/_get_scene helpers are explicitly internal-only and do not undermine the facade claim. REQ-FS-APP-001 (three execution modes via CLI) anchors at main.py L20 with choices=['dashboard','sim','builder']; the v0.2 rewording correctly decouples the requirement from specific argparse flag values. All RTM anchor file paths in section 5.2.1 for these requirements exist. The high-level invariant framing is appropriate for an adoption RS. Document content sections 1-9 are complete; executable placeholders in 10-12 correctly remain. No request-updates from UI domain perspective.

REQ-FS-SIM-002 (Compiler Bridge) is factually incomplete. The statement says the Compiler 'shall translate Sketch geometry into static Simulation atoms (compiled atoms)', but the Compiler actually produces TWO kinds of atoms: static atoms (is_static=1) for non-dynamic entities AND tethered atoms (is_static=3) for entities with dynamic=True. See flow-state/engine/compiler.py lines 236 and 266, and the file's own docstring (lines 9-22) which documents both Static, Tethered, and Handle particle types. Tethered atoms are not an incidental detail — they are the mechanism by which the Compiler enables two-way coupling between CAD geometry and physics (the 'tether spring' system documented in compiler.py and exercised by tether_entity_idx, tether_local_pos, tether_stiffness in simulation.py). Omitting this from the RS produces the same kind of CLAUDE.md-vs-code drift the v0.2 revision was already correcting elsewhere, and it would leave future CRs touching the tether system without an anchor requirement. Suggest restating REQ-FS-SIM-002 along the lines of: 'The Compiler shall translate Sketch geometry into Simulation atoms (static atoms for non-dynamic entities, tethered atoms for dynamic entities) and shall rebuild those atoms when the Scene reports that geometry has changed.' Other SIM-domain content checked: REQ-FS-SIM-001 (flat NumPy arrays, velocity Verlet via integrate_n_steps, cell-list spatial hashing via build_neighbor_list) is verified against flow-state/engine/simulation.py and flow-state/engine/physics_core.py. RTM anchor file map for SIM rows is appropriate.

Reviewed from the orchestration-domain perspective (Scene, Commands, ToolContext, Compiler bridge orchestration). Findings:

REQ-FS-ARCH-001 (Air Gap): Accurate. Verified scene.execute() is the sole mutation gateway and routes through CommandQueue (core/scene.py L184-204).

REQ-FS-ARCH-002 (Command Pattern): Accurate. Verified Command ABC in core/command_base.py declares execute() and undo() as @abstractmethod and carries the historize flag. Optional redo()/merge()/changes_topology surface area is consistent with the statement.

REQ-FS-ARCH-003 (Scene/Session Split): Accurate, including the v0.2 correction. Verified Scene owns Sketch, Simulation, Compiler, ProcessObjects, and CommandQueue (core/scene.py L66-97); Session owns camera, selection, focused_element, current tool, and mode (core/session.py L46-99). interaction_data correctly lives on Sketch (referenced as self.sketch.interaction_data in scene.update L324) — the v0.2 removal of it from the Session list is correct.

REQ-FS-ARCH-004 (ToolContext Facade): Accurate. Verified ToolContext exposes execute(), read-only view/geometry queries, and transient interaction state, while keeping the app reference private (_app) to prevent bypass (core/tool_context.py L33-96).

REQ-FS-SIM-002 (Compiler Bridge, orchestration aspect): Accurate. Scene owns the Compiler and triggers compiler.rebuild() via Scene.rebuild() when topology is dirty; the orchestrated update loop honors the dirty flags (core/scene.py L292-296, L421-429).

RTM anchor file map for the orchestration domain (scene.py, commands.py, command_base.py, tool_context.py, session.py, compiler.py): All files exist at the current flow-state HEAD.

Lightweight scope is appropriate. The 12 high-level requirements capture essential structural and behavioral invariants without over-committing to granular feature claims that would be revised on the first feature CR. The SDLC-CQ precedent is sound.

Informational note (not blocking, not a request-updates): the actual scene.update() loop is now an 8-step orchestration (topology check, driver update, entity-array sync, physics coupling sub-loop, constraint solve, post-constraint sync, process objects, physics step) rather than the simpler 5-step loop described in CLAUDE.md Section 7. The RS deliberately does not lock down the step order at the requirement level, which is appropriate for v1.0. The author has already flagged the broader CLAUDE.md drift in the revision_summary; the proposed follow-up CR can address it.

No blocking issues from the orchestration domain. Recommend.

QA pre-review of CR-111 v0.3 — COMPLIANT.

FRONTMATTER VERIFIED:
- title: 'Adopt Flow State into QMS Governance' (present, non-empty)
- revision_summary: present, descriptive — documents v0.3 resolution of tu_sim feedback (REQ-FS-SIM-002 distinguishing static atoms is_static=1 vs tethered atoms is_static=3) and retains v0.2 corrections (REQ-FS-APP-001 mode wording, REQ-FS-UI-002 input layer order System->Modal->Global->HUD, REQ-FS-ARCH-003 interaction_data scope correction, sec 2.2/5.1 harmonization on 5 domain groups).

STRUCTURE VERIFIED:
- All 12 standard CR sections present (Purpose through References).
- Section 7.4/7.5 omission is explicitly justified per TEMPLATE-CR guidance for document-only CRs.

CONTENT VERIFIED:
- 12 requirements in sec 5.1 (4 ARCH + 3 CAD + 2 SIM + 2 UI + 1 APP) match the anchor file map in sec 5.2.1 (12 rows).
- 10 EIs in sec 10 map cleanly to the 5 implementation phases in sec 9.
- Pre-execution and post-execution baselines (EI-1, EI-10) per SOP-004 sec 5 are present.
- VR column empty across all EIs is consistent with the document-only character (no behavioral code changes).
- Qualified baseline language in sec 5.3 is appropriately conditional ('expected a26f7fb; re-confirmed during EI-5'), not a 'TBD' placeholder.
- No content placeholders (TBD/TODO/FIXME) found in authored sections. Standard execution-table markers in sec 10/11 are template-standard and filled during execution.

REVIEW TEAM ASSIGNED:
tu_sketch (CAD/sketch domain — REQ-FS-CAD-001/002/003), tu_sim (simulation/compiler — REQ-FS-SIM-001/002), tu_scene (architecture/orchestrator — REQ-FS-ARCH-001/002/003/004 and REQ-FS-APP-001), tu_ui (UI/input — REQ-FS-UI-001/002 and REQ-FS-APP-001 mode selector). This matches the team proposed in sec 9.2 of the CR and provides full coverage across all 5 RS domain groups.

QA recommends. Awaiting TU reviews.

v0.3 review (SIM domain). The v0.2 request-updates concern is fully addressed: REQ-FS-SIM-002 now correctly distinguishes static atoms (is_static=1) from tethered atoms (is_static=3) and explicitly identifies the tethered mechanism as the basis for two-way CAD<->Physics coupling. Verified against flow-state/engine/compiler.py (lines 9-12, 65-66, 218, 236, 266) and flow-state/engine/physics_core.py (lines 204, 329-355): the documented atom-type taxonomy and tether semantics match the implementation. REQ-FS-SIM-001 is also accurate -- flat NumPy arrays per DOD (engine/simulation.py 50-69), velocity-Verlet integration (engine/physics_core.py integrate_n_steps), and spatial hashing (build_neighbor_list / spatial_sort with cell_size). All SIM-domain anchor files exist in the cited locations. Both SIM requirements are appropriately high-level, atomic, and accurately describe behavioral invariants of the existing system, consistent with the lightweight SDLC-CQ-style adoption pattern. No SIM-domain issues remain.

CAD/Geometry domain review (tu_sketch): Recommend. The RS accurately describes the CAD subsystem at an appropriate level of abstraction. REQ-FS-CAD-001 (entities/constraints), REQ-FS-CAD-002 (PBD with hybrid Python/Numba backend, F9-switchable), and REQ-FS-CAD-003 (mouse-as-constraint Servo) all match the implementation in flow-state/model/. The v0.3 correction to REQ-FS-SIM-002 distinguishing static atoms (is_static=1) from tethered atoms (is_static=3) precisely matches engine/compiler.py and resolves the CLAUDE.md drift tu_sim flagged. The v0.2 correction relocating interaction_data from Session to Sketch in REQ-FS-ARCH-003 matches the actual ownership in model/sketch.py. The REQ-FS-ARCH series correctly characterizes the Air Gap, Command Pattern, Scene/Session split, and ToolContext facade as they pertain to CAD-domain mutations. Minor observation (non-blocking): REQ-FS-CAD-001 names lines and circles as primary entities; Point is also a first-class entity class used throughout the constraint and solver code, though it has no public add_point() API in Sketch. The RTM qualitative-proof verification can clarify this if needed; not worth a request-updates. Lightweight inspection-only adoption mirroring SDLC-CQ is appropriate for a pre-test-suite system.

UI-domain review of CR-111 v0.3.

Verified UI-domain requirements against flow-state HEAD a26f7fb:

REQ-FS-UI-001 (Hierarchical UI Tree with Overlays): Confirmed. ui/ui_manager.py defines UIManager with self.root, self.overlays list, register_overlay/unregister_overlay API, and _draw_overlays() called separately to render after the main tree (correct Z-ordering). ui/ui_widgets.py defines OverlayProvider as an explicit protocol class; Dropdown subclasses it (line 712) and registers/unregisters via overlay lifecycle hooks. Anchor files exist; statement accurately captures the implementation.

REQ-FS-UI-002 (Input Chain of Responsibility): Confirmed. ui/input_handler.py handle_input() executes in the order System -> Modals -> Global -> HUD as the requirement states. v0.2's correction to put Modal before Global is right and matches the code (dialogs must capture keyboard before global hotkeys fire, e.g., typing 'b' inside a dialog field must not switch to brush). Modal-stack interaction-state reset is implemented symmetrically: app/app_controller.py push_modal() (line 58-59) and pop_modal() (line 74-75) both call self.app.ui.root.reset_interaction_state(); ui/ui_widgets.py defines the recursive reset on UIContainer (line 268). Statement accurately captures behavior.

REQ-FS-ARCH-004 (ToolContext Facade) - cross-domain spot-check: core/tool_context.py exposes execute()/discard(), view properties (zoom/pan/mode), interaction state, geometry queries, and coordinate transforms. Tools receive this facade rather than the app. Tool base class still sets self.app = ctx._app for backward compat per CLAUDE.md §5.5 - this is a tool-side migration concern, not a defect of the requirement statement, and is correctly framed as a v1.0 capture of intent.

REQ-FS-ARCH-003 (Scene/Session Split): core/session.py owns mode, state, current_tool/tools, focused_element, camera, selection, constraint_builder, status - all five elements named in the requirement are present. interaction_data correctly removed in v0.2 (lives on Sketch, not Session).

Two minor non-blocking observations for awareness (already covered by the queued follow-up CR for CLAUDE.md/code-doc drift mentioned in v0.3's revision_summary):

1. ui/input_handler.py module docstring (lines 4-9) lists the layers as 'System / Global / Modals / HUD' which contradicts both the actual handle_input() execution order AND REQ-FS-UI-002. Recommend the follow-up CR include this docstring in its drift cleanup.

2. Tool base class's self.app = ctx._app backward-compat passthrough (ui/tools.py line 87) is the kind of thing a future CR may want to formally retire once tool-side _app references are eliminated; not in scope for this Adoption CR.

UI-domain anchor files in §5.2.1 all exist at HEAD a26f7fb. The 12-requirement scope is appropriately lightweight for an adoption-style RS, mirrors the SDLC-CQ pattern as claimed, and the qualified-baseline plan (tag FLOW-STATE-1.0 on flow-state/main) is sound and unambiguous.

Recommend approval.

Reviewed from the orchestration domain perspective. Verified all four ARCH requirements against the actual code: REQ-FS-ARCH-001 (Air Gap) holds in core/scene.py and core/commands.py; REQ-FS-ARCH-002 (Command Pattern) matches core/command_base.py (execute, undo, historize); REQ-FS-ARCH-003 (Scene/Session Split) matches core/scene.py and core/session.py — Scene owns Sketch, Simulation, Compiler, ProcessObjects, CommandQueue; Session owns mode, camera, selection, focused_element, current_tool, status. REQ-FS-ARCH-004 (ToolContext Facade) verifies cleanly in core/tool_context.py: tools receive ctx, app reference is private (_app), execute() is the only mutation path. REQ-FS-SIM-002 (Compiler Bridge) is correctly anchored on engine/compiler.py + core/scene.py — confirmed scene.rebuild() drives compiler.rebuild(), and scene.update() dispatches between full rebuild and fast sync via the topology-dirty flag.

One observation, offered as a preference rather than a blocker: REQ-FS-ARCH-002 mentions only the historize flag, but the Command base class (core/command_base.py) also carries (a) a supersede flag central to live-preview drag patterns and (b) a changes_topology class attribute that scene.execute() inspects to choose between full Compiler rebuild vs a fast sync_entity_arrays path (scene.py lines 200-203). The changes_topology attribute is specifically what couples REQ-FS-ARCH-002 to REQ-FS-SIM-002 — a Command author who omits it on a topology-affecting command produces a real orchestration defect (the Compiler bridge silently goes stale). The RS framing as written is accurate but partial; a future granular CR could reasonably extend REQ-FS-ARCH-002 to mention the topology-classification contract. I am not requesting changes for v1.0 because the RS philosophy in §5.1 is explicitly lightweight and the omission is a coverage choice consistent with that philosophy.

REQ-FS-UI-002 input order (System → Modal → Global → HUD) verified against ui/input_handler.py lines 114-129 — matches exactly. The known CLAUDE.md drift on the orchestration-loop step count (loop is now 8 steps in scene.update, not 5) is appropriately deferred to a follow-up CR per the v0.3 revision_summary; no action needed in this CR.

Recommend approval.
